question.text: When the spot price of Nifty is 17,500 and a trader holds a call option with strike price 17,500, the option is:

answer.text: At-the-Money (ATM) because the spot price equals the strike price
In-the-Money (ITM) because the option has been purchased
Out-of-the-Money (OTM) because there is no profit
Neither ITM nor OTM - it is called a neutral option

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When Spot Price = Strike Price, the option is AT-THE-MONEY (ATM). This applies to BOTH calls and puts. - ATM Call: S = X (17,500 = 17,500) - ATM Put: S = X (17,500 = 17,500)
**The Trap:** ATM does not mean "no profit." An ATM option still has time value and can become profitable.
**Cross-Exam Context:** ATM options have the highest time value and the highest gamma among all strike prices.
**Key Takeaway:** ATM: Spot = Strike. True for both calls and puts. ATM options have maximum time value.
